message:
fix: race panic masking, notify O(N^2) scan, Kafka producer-per-send, tower bridge Cx

race.rs:
- Reorder result checking in race2_to_result and race_all_to_result
  to check loser panics BEFORE returning Ok(winner) — previously a
  loser panic would be silently dropped if the winner succeeded,
  masking real failures in concurrent task graphs

notify.rs:
- Add scan_start to WaiterSlab so notify_one() and pass_baton() skip
  already-processed entries, converting O(N^2) sequential notify
  workloads to O(N) amortized
- Inline the notify_bug_test.rs module directly into notify.rs tests
  (the separate file is removed)
- New test: notify_one_lost_if_followed_by_broadcast_and_cancel

kafka.rs:
- Store ThreadedProducer as a field in KafkaProducer::new() instead of
  creating a fresh producer per send()/send_batch()/flush() call —
  the per-send approach created a producer with zero in-flight messages,
  making flush() a no-op and likely dropping undelivered messages when
  the producer was dropped before delivery completed

cx.rs:
- Add PhantomData<*mut ()> to CurrentCxGuard to make it !Send — the
  guard restores a thread-local on drop, so sending it to another
  thread would corrupt that thread's Cx stack

tower_bridge.rs:
- Replace manual Cx set + cancellation check with with_tokio_context()
  which installs the Cx on every poll and checks cancellation, fixing
  a race where cancellation during the response future was missed

dns/resolver.rs:
- Test now uses async lookup_ip() path and validates InvalidHost error

doctor/mod.rs:
- Refactor mutable-then-overwrite pattern to if/else let bindings,
  eliminating #[allow(clippy::useless_let_if_seq)]

trace/integrity.rs:
- Hoist MAX_METADATA_LEN/MAX_EVENT_LEN to module-level constants

pool.rs:
- Add DropTrackedResource for testing resource cleanup on
  close-while-create

doctor_remediation_unit_harness.rs:
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on idempotency_key

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
